headline: The visual vocabulary
for building the web
hero.subtitle: Live samples of motion components, blocks and design styles. See them, copy the source, or take the words to tell your AI.

landing.installNote: Built on Framer Motion. Distributed via shadcn.
landing.spaceStylesDesc: See a style, take the words for AI.
landing.spacePalettesDesc: Recognize the colors you want, hexes included.
landing.spaceSectionsDesc: Landing-page anatomy: that top block is a hero.
landing.spaceScrollDesc: See eight scroll narratives inside isolated live viewports.
landing.spaceLayoutsDesc: Dress a complete Agent workbench in different product languages.
landing.spacePatternsDesc: See how a whole page divides space, then copy the CSS skeleton.
landing.spaceAtomsDesc: Curves, radii, spacing, lines, type, icons, and backgrounds — copy the truth in one place.
landing.spaceStudioDesc: Dial in the atoms, then export your own design system.

atoms.title: Atoms
atoms.intro: The quantities beneath the button — curves, radii, shadows, and tracking. See the difference, learn the name, and copy the value itself so AI can use it directly instead of rewriting a description.
atoms.reducedMotionStatic: Static start and end frames shown for reduced motion
atoms.motionComparatorHint: Click any track to replay it, or replay everything side by side. With reduced motion enabled, tracks show static start and end frames.
atoms.radiusComparatorTitle: One radius reshapes every surface live
atoms.radiusComparatorHint: Pick one of eight steps and watch the card, button, badge, and input change together. Copy the current value directly.
atoms.shadowComparatorTitle: A shadow ladder from flush to prominent
atoms.shadowComparatorHint: Four elevations sit on real surfaces. The current theme selects the light or dark truth; click a surface to copy its full box-shadow.
atoms.shadowAntiSlop: Anti-slop: establish hierarchy with hairlines and shallow shadows first. Do not stack heavy black shadows.
atoms.shapeValuesTitle: The complete radius and shadow scales
atoms.typeComparatorTitle: One specimen, a full type swap
atoms.typeComparatorHint: Switch six display / body / mono system stacks with no webfont loading; headings, paragraphs, code, and captions all change together.
atoms.typeScaleHint: Six steps render at their real size, each with an exact font size, line height, and tracking value.
atoms.typeValuesTitle: Font pairings and the type scale
atoms.densityComparatorHint: Each list uses the same content and structure; only row height and horizontal padding change. Select a density to highlight its fixed parameters.
atoms.spacingAntiSlop: Anti-slop: use this one 4px-grid scale across the product. Never introduce off-scale values such as 13px or 18px.
atoms.linesComparatorTitle: Five structural lines in light and dark
atoms.linesComparatorHint: The same small card shows paired light and dark truths. The focus-ring card contains a real button; use Tab to see the required keyboard focus.
atoms.focusTabHint: Press Tab to focus the button and inspect the 2px ring with a 2px offset.
atoms.linesAntiSlop: Anti-slop: never mix a hairline ring and a 1px border on elements at the same hierarchy level.
atoms.iconsComparatorHint: Home, search, gear, heart, and star are all hand-drawn SVGs. Compare stroke weight, rounding, fill, duotone, and pixel construction row by row.
atoms.iconsAntiSlop: Anti-slop: use one icon style across the product. Never mix outline and filled icons in the same bar.
atoms.iconSpecLabel: Click to copy the AI-ready specification
atoms.backgroundsComparatorTitle: Nine static textures, one paste away
atoms.backgroundsComparatorHint: Each tile follows the current light or dark theme. Click a tile to copy that theme's complete CSS declaration.
atoms.backgroundsIntro: Paste-ready background texture recipes. Pure CSS, zero dependencies; for dynamic shader backgrounds, see the <webgl>webgl-background</webgl> component.
atoms.backgroundCssLabel: Click to copy the complete CSS declaration

studio.title: Studio
studio.intro: Turn a few atomic dials and watch a real workbench change character live. When it feels right, copy the complete DESIGN.md and token set so AI can build from the same system.
studio.sampleCardBody: Color, typography, and geometry tokens are working together across one component set.
studio.exportTitle: Hand the current system to AI
studio.exportHint: Each button copies the complete output for the current dials. Change one setting and the next copy updates with it.

sections.title: The anatomy of a landing page
sections.intro: From navbar to footer, meet every block in top-to-bottom order: what it's called, what it looks like, when to pick which shape, and how to say it to AI.
sections.promptHint: Section name + structure checklist + forbidden list, one per shape.

scroll.title: Ways a page can tell a story while you scroll
scroll.intro: Eight familiar scroll techniques that are hard to name. Every live sample stays inside its own device viewport, paired with precise names, use cases, a copy-ready recipe, and words for AI.
scroll.isolationHint: Page scroll does not drive the samples. Move your pointer into a frame and scroll inside it.

patterns.title: How a whole page divides its space
patterns.intro: Eight structural skeletons for arranging headers, sidebars, main content, context panes, and canvases. Each labeled wireframe is built with real Grid or Flexbox and comes with responsive guidance, copy-ready CSS, and precise words for AI.
patterns.openLayouts: Open the complete workbench in Design Systems

layouts.title: An immersive Agent workbench showcase
layouts.intro: The same workbench, dressed in different product languages — see it, recognize it, and say it to AI. Every entry lives inside a complete task, not a static swatch sheet.
layouts.stageHint: A simulated stage assembled from real components: resize or collapse the side panes, open the artifact preview, switch source or versions, or type and send a message.
layouts.anatomyTitle: What this workbench is made of
layouts.anatomyIntro: Hover or focus a wireframe block to mark the matching region in the live stage above; click it to open that building block's component docs.
layouts.anatomyHint: Hover to link · click for docs
layouts.promptHint: Named system + concrete visual moves + a forbidden list, copy-ready.
layouts.designMdDescription: Copy a short DESIGN.md so future Agents keep working inside the same structure, hierarchy, and motion constraints.

palettes.title: Color palettes you have to see
palettes.intro: The same page, dressed in different palettes. Recognize the colors you want, then copy "say this to AI" — every hex comes with its role.
palettes.moreLink: Need more than these 16? Generate endless palettes on <link>Huemint</link> — pick one you like, then hand it to AI with the role assignments here.
palettes.promptHint: Hex values + role assignments + a forbidden list beat mood words alone.

styles.title: Design styles you have to see
styles.intro: The same page, dressed in different styles. Recognize the one you want, then copy "say this to AI" straight into your AI tool.
styles.promptHint: Style name + concrete visual moves + forbidden list beats naming the style alone.
styles.demoSubtitle: Describe the feeling you want. It handles the rest.
styles.demoCardOneBody: From description to live in minutes.
styles.demoCardTwoBody: You pick the style, we hand you the words.

detail.installHint: Add it with the shadcn CLI, or copy the source manually.

footer.tagline: A visual vocabulary for the web — components, blocks & design styles. Copy-paste via the shadcn registry.
